versions.label: LSB
versions.text: "This is good and acceptable in the sight of God our Savior, who desires all men to be saved and to come to the knowledge of the truth."

context_lines: 1 Timothy 2:3-4 should be read within the flow of its chapter and the larger argument of 1 Timothy.
explanation_lines: God's revealed will centers on the salvation of souls, the sanctification of believers, gratitude in all circumstances, and eternal life for those who believe. These are not mysteries — they are stated plainly and repeatedly throughout Scripture.

God's providential will — His specific purposes for individual circumstances, situations, and events, which we often cannot know in advance. This is what Paul could not see when he asked three times for the thorn to be removed. This is what Abraham tried to engineer in Egypt instead of trusting. It is the path God is clearing that Proverbs 3:5-6 tells us we are not equipped to navigate on our own.
